heading: Stay of proceedings for taking evidence before suit is filed
label: 8894 Stay of proceedings for taking evidence before suit is filed
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7724(less applicability to duration of stay).46 U.S.C. 792(less applicability to duration of stay), .July 3, 1944, ch. 399, § 258 Stat. 724.46 U.S.C. 796, .July 3, 1944, ch. 399, § 658 Stat. 726In subsection (b) the words “upon a claim against the United States” and “as to any proceedings by or on behalf of such claimant for the taking of a deposition or the production of evidence in connection with or in relation to such claim” are omitted as surplusage.
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section and substituted “section 8891” for “section 7721” in introductory provisions of subsec. (a) and in subsec. (b).Pub. L. 115–232section 7724 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.text: the granting of a dedimus potestatem to take depositions;
a direction to take depositions in perpetuam rei memoriam; or
the taking of depositions or production of evidence pursuant to such dedimus potestatem or direction, or pursuant to any other proceedings for the purpose;

children.label: b
children.text: The time during which a claimant may file suit of the type described in is computed by excluding the time during which a stay under this section or any extension of such a stay is in effect.section 8891 of this title
